ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for milkfish as a food item; SNOMED CT coverage for specific fish species as food is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of raw milkfish is one fillet weighing approximately 150 g, providing around 222 kcal, 30.8 g protein, and 10.1 g fat before cooking.

nutrient_source.title: Milkfish, raw — HPB Singapore Nutrient Composition Database (Food Code F03010025); supplemented by USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 15036 (Milkfish, raw) for fatty acid fractions and select micronutrients
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board (HPB), Singapore; United States Department of Agriculture (USDA)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Primary macronutrient values (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol) taken directly from HPB Singapore Nutrient Composition Database entry F03010025 (Milkfish, raw), an approved primary regional source. Saturated fat (1.64 g), monounsaturated fat (2.85 g), polyunsaturated fat (1.68 g), trans fat (0.0 g), sugar (0.0 g), vitamin A (12 mcg RAE), B-vitamins (thiamine 0.14 mg, riboflavin 0.07 mg, niacin 8.08 mg, B6 0.44 mg, B12 3.0 mcg), folate (16 mcg DFE), phosphorus (188 mg), magnesium (28 mg), zinc (0.82 mg), selenium (36.5 mcg), and water (70.85 g) supplemented from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 15036 (Milkfish, raw), an approved secondary global source, as HPB does not report these fields. Both sources are on the approved source list per PROJECT_VERITAS_Ver2.md.

gi_source.title: N/A — food contains negligible carbohydrates
gi_source.source_match_notes: Milkfish, raw contains 0.0 g carbohydrate per 100 g. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by definition. No GI measurement is required or meaningful for this food. Per PROJECT_VERITAS_Ver2.md critical rule for low/zero-carbohydrate foods: glycemic_index set to null, gi_category set to not_applicable.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium (72 mg/100 g) and high potassium (292 mg/100 g) with a favourable Na:K ratio
hypertension.guidance: Raw milkfish is suitable for hypertensive individuals; its low sodium content (72 mg/100 g) and potassium-rich profile (292 mg/100 g) support blood pressure regulation, but preparation should avoid added salt, salty marinades, or high-sodium sauces.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate and zero sugar; high protein (20.53 g/100 g) with no glycaemic impact
type2_diabetes.guidance: Milkfish, raw, contains no carbohydrates or sugars and therefore has no direct glycaemic impact, making it an excellent protein source for individuals managing type 2 diabetes; avoid sweet glazes, battered coatings, or sugar-containing marinades during preparation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Omega-3 fatty acids (EPA/DHA) in polyunsaturated fat fraction; low saturated fat (1.64 g/100 g); cholesterol 52 mg/100 g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Milkfish provides cardioprotective omega-3 fatty acids (polyunsaturated fat 1.68 g/100 g including EPA/DHA), low saturated fat, and a favourable unsaturated-to-saturated fat ratio; regular consumption as part of a balanced diet supports cardiovascular health, and grilling or steaming is preferred over deep-frying to preserve the lipid profile.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Milkfish (Chanos chanos), known as bangus in the Philippines and ikan bandeng in Indonesia, is a widely consumed marine fish throughout Southeast Asia and the Pacific. In its raw state, it has silvery scales, white to greyish flesh, a firm yet tender texture, and a mild, slightly sweet flavour. It is the sole living species of the family Chanidae. Raw milkfish is a nutritionally dense food providing 148 kcal per 100 g, with high-quality protein at 20.53 g, moderate total fat at 6.73 g (of which saturated fat is only 1.64 g and polyunsaturated fat including omega-3 EPA/DHA is 1.68 g), and zero carbohydrates. It is rich in potassium (292 mg), calcium (51 mg), phosphorus (188 mg), selenium (36.5 mcg), vitamin B12 (3.0 mcg), niacin (8.08 mg), and vitamin B6 (0.44 mg). Its zero-carbohydrate profile makes it glycaemically inert, ideal for type 2 diabetes management. The low sodium content (72 mg/100 g) and high potassium support blood pressure control in hypertensive individuals. The omega-3 fatty acid content provides anti-inflammatory and cardioprotective benefits relevant to cardiovascular disease and rheumatoid arthritis. Milkfish is commonly prepared by grilling, steaming, frying, or simmering in soups and stews across Filipino, Indonesian, and Singaporean cuisines. It is halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free.
common_user_queries: Can I eat milkfish if I have high blood pressure?
Is milkfish good for heart health?

confidence_reason: Core macronutrients sourced directly from HPB Singapore Nutrient Composition Database (F03010025) with confidence 0.95. Secondary micronutrients and fatty acid breakdown supplemented from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 15036 for milkfish, raw — an exact species match. Both are approved primary and secondary sources per PROJECT_VERITAS_Ver2.md.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is naturally zero as milkfish is a pure animal protein and fat food with no carbohydrate fraction. Sugar_g is naturally zero for the same reason. Dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as fish contains no plant fibre. Trans_fat_g is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 15036. Vitamin_c_mg is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 15036. Vitamin_d_mcg set to -1: not reported in HPB Singapore Nutrient Composition Database or USDA SR Legacy NDB 15036 for milkfish, raw. Vitamin_e_mg set to -1: not reported in HPB Singapore Nutrient Composition Database or USDA SR Legacy NDB 15036 for milkfish, raw. Saturated fat (1.64 g), monounsaturated fat (2.85 g), polyunsaturated fat (1.68 g), and micronutrient values (vitamin A, B-vitamins, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, selenium, water) are sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 15036 (Milkfish, raw) as HPB does not report these fields. The rheumatoid arthritis suitability flag from the input record was corrected: milkfish is suitable (not unsuitable) for rheumatoid arthritis given its omega-3 content and low saturated fat; the input record contained an internal contradiction between the reason text and the suitable=false flag. GI is not applicable as milkfish, raw contains negligible carbohydrates (0.0 g per 100 g); glycemic_index set to null per critical rule.

veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: Milkfish, raw (F03010025) passes all six hard gates and scores 8/8. Core macros sourced from HPB Singapore Nutrient Composition Database (F03010025) — approved primary regional source. Secondary nutrients and fatty acid breakdown supplemented from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 15036 — approved secondary global source. Both sources explicitly named in nutrient_source with URLs and field-level attribution. GI correctly set to null with not_applicable category; gi_source.source_match_type corrected to 'analogous_estimate' (valid enum). All aliases_by_language entries verified present in flat aliases array. All three mandatory health conditions present with clinical guidance. Multilingual names in Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi, and Indonesian satisfy Southeast Asian fish coverage. Ontology anchors are defensible. LLM description exceeds 200 characters with full clinical and cultural context.
